It's a hundred dollars per sale And if you've got a small sample size Facebook can't properly optimize Okay, so something we've talked about a few times or I say properly they can't optimally optimize if that makes any sense So the approach to this is first of all, don't make decisions If you can if you can until a learning phase exit that learning phase exit tends to happen after 50 actions Within a week so it could happen sooner than the week But far too often what I've seen is Facebook isn't done hasn't found the best way to optimize yet And so the results aren't great in those first couple days, but then they improve so if you make If you stop the campaign before Facebook has a chance to improve you may have made a mistake Also consider increasing your budget if you can to get an adequate sample size and Consider aggregating your ad sets or budget to increase that sample size too. So for example, maybe you're not getting a good sample size of conversions or You know sales or registrations Because you have five different ad sets all trying to accomplish this goal Well, if you combine them if you aggregate them You'll be able to get that sample size out of a single ad set and Facebook made them be able to properly optimize for you So the learning phase is something we've talked about a few times and some of these factors It's something that's important to understand The Facebook wants you to try to get at least 50 of a particular action Within a week and if you can't that low sample size is going to impact Facebook's optimization If you can you may be able to get better results So there's an advantage to getting that sample size and you should try strive for that at all times
